is required to: It is the responsibility of the Requester to make sure the Requester has the necessary authority to publish to the request topic, and to subscribe to the Topic Name that the Requester sets in the Response Topic property.
It is the responsibility of the Responder to make sure the Responder has the authority to subscribe to the request topic and publish to the Response Topic.
is recommended that: it is recommended that Servers implement topic authorization

5 Security (non-normative) -> 5.4 Implem.data:
{
    "would normally choose to validate": "SHOULD",
    "avoids implementation difficulties": "SHOULD",
    "protects applications from having to process": "SHOULD",
    "removes some security exposures": "SHOULD",
    "could permit such exploits": "SHOULD NOT"
}

MUST: MUST assign an unused Packet Identifier when the sender has a new Application Message to publish
MUST send a PUBLISH packet containing this Packet Identifier with QoS 2 and DUP flag set to 0
MUST treat the PUBLISH packet as unacknowledged until the sender has received the corresponding PUBREC packet from the receiver
MUST send a PUBREL packet when the sender receives a PUBREC packet from the receiver with a Reason Code value less than 0x80
MUST contain the same Packet Identifier as the original PUBLISH packet
MUST treat the PUBREL packet as unacknowledged until the sender has received the corresponding PUBCOMP packet from the receiver
MUST NOT re-send the PUBLISH once the sender has sent the corresponding PUBREL packet
MUST NOT apply Message expiry if a PUBLISH packet has been sent
MUST respond with a PUBREC containing the Packet Identifier from the incoming PUBLISH packet, having accepted ownership of the Application Message
MUST treat any subsequent PUBLISH packet that contains that Packet Identifier as being a new Application Message
MUST acknowledge any subsequent PUBLISH packet with the same Packet Identifier by sending a PUBREC
MUST NOT cause duplicate messages to be delivered to any onward recipients in this case
MUST respond to a PUBREL packet by sending a PUBCOMP packet containing the same Packet Identifier as the PUBREL
MUST treat any subsequent PUBLISH packet that contains that Packet Identifier as being a new Application Message
MUST continue the QoS 2 acknowledgement sequence even if the receiver has applied message expiry

Mandatory: should be revoked
should allow for session renegotiation
Recommended: can use TLS session resumption

5 Security (non-normative) -> 5.4 Implem.data:
{
    "might monitor": "Server implementations might monitor Client behavior to detect potential security incidents",
    "might monitor for": "Server implementations might monitor for repeated connection attempts, repeated authentication attempts, abnormal termination of connections, topic scanning (attempts to send or subscribe to many topics), sending undeliverable messages (no subscribers to the topics), and clients that connect but do not send data",
    "might close": "Server implementations might close the network connection of clients that breach the server's security rules",
    "might implement": "Server implementations detecting unwelcome behavior might implement a dynamic block list based on identifiers such as IP address or Client Identifier",
    "might use": "Deployments might use network-level controls (where available) to implement rate limiting or blocking based on IP address or other information"
}

failure to do X will result in Y

7 Conformance -> 7.1 Conformance clauses.data:
{
    "conforms to this specification only if": "mandatory",
    "satisfies all the statements below": "mandatory",
    "matches the format described": "mandatory",
    "follows the Topic matching rules": "mandatory",
    "satisfies the MUST level requirements": "mandatory",
    "does not require the use of any extensions defined outside of the specification": "prohibited"
}

Mandatory: MUST be sent in WebSocket binary data frames
MUST close the Network Connection
MUST NOT assume that MQTT Control Packets are aligned on WebSocket frame boundaries
MUST include mqtt in the list of WebSocket Sub Protocols the Client offers
MUST be mqtt
Prohibited: MUST NOT assume that MQTT Control Packets are aligned on WebSocket frame boundaries

3 MQTT Control Packets -> 3.8 SUBSCRIBE.data:
{
    "MUST respond with a SUBACK packet": "When the Server receives a SUBSCRIBE packet from a Client, the Server MUST respond with a SUBACK packet [MQTT-3.8.4-1]",
    "MUST have the same Packet Identifier": "The SUBACK packet MUST have the same Packet Identifier as the SUBSCRIBE packet that it is acknowledging [MQTT-3.8.4-2]",
    "MUST replace that existing Subscription": "If a Server receives a SUBSCRIBE packet containing a Topic Filter that is identical to a Non-shared Subscription's Topic Filter for the current Session, then the Server MUST replace that existing Subscription with a new Subscription [MQTT-3.8.4-3]",
    "MUST be re-sent": "If the Retain Handling option is 0, any existing retained messages matching the Topic Filter MUST be re-sent",
    "MUST NOT be lost": "Application Messages MUST NOT be lost due to replacing the Subscription [MQTT-3.8.4-4]",
    "MUST handle that packet as if it had received a sequence": "If a Server receives a SUBSCRIBE packet that contains multiple Topic Filters, the Server MUST handle that packet as if it had received a sequence of multiple SUBSCRIBE packets",
    "MUST combine their responses": "the Server combines their responses into a single SUBACK response [MQTT-3.8.4-5]",
    "MUST contain a Reason Code": "The SUBACK packet sent by the Server to the Client MUST contain a Reason Code for each Topic Filter/Subscription Option pair [MQTT-3.8.4-6]",
    "MUST either show the maximum QoS": "The Reason Code MUST either show the maximum QoS that was granted for that Subscription or indicate that the subscription failed [MQTT-3.8.4-7]",
    "MUST be the minimum": "The QoS of Application Messages sent in response to a Subscription MUST be the minimum of the QoS of the originally published message and the Maximum QoS granted by the Server [MQTT-3.8.4-8]",
    "should never send a duplicate": "the Server should never send a duplicate of that Message",
    "need not use the same set": "The Server need not use the same set of Subscription Identifiers in the retransmitted PUBLISH packet",
    "is not allowed to revert": "The Server is not allowed to revert to the old identifier after it has sent a PUBLISH packet containing the new one"
}

MUST: MUST respond with the packet as determined by the QoS in the PUBLISH Packet
MUST deliver the message to the Client respecting the maximum QoS of all the matching subscriptions
MUST send those Subscription Identifiers in the message which is published as the result of the subscriptions
MUST include in the PUBLISH packet the Subscription Identifiers for all matching subscriptions which have Subscription Identifiers
MUST send, in each of them, the Subscription Identifier of the matching subscription if it has a Subscription Identifier
MUST NOT send more than Receive Maximum QoS 1 and QoS 2 PUBLISH packets for which it has not received PUBACK, PUBCOMP, or PUBREC with a Reason Code of 128 or greater from the Server
MUST NOT delay the sending of any packets other than PUBLISH packets due to having sent Receive Maximum PUBLISH packets without receiving acknowledgements for them
MUST NOT send more than Receive Maximum QoS 1 and QoS 2 PUBLISH packets for which the Server has not received PUBACK, PUBCOMP, or PUBREC with a Reason Code of 128 or greater from the Client
MUST NOT delay the sending of any packets other than PUBLISH packets due to having sent Receive Maximum PUBLISH packets without receiving acknowledgements for them
MUST NOT: MUST NOT contain a Subscription Identifier
MUST NOT send more than Receive Maximum QoS 1 and QoS 2 PUBLISH packets for which it has not received PUBACK, PUBCOMP, or PUBREC with a Reason Code of 128 or greater from the Server
MUST NOT delay the sending of any packets other than PUBLISH packets due to having sent Receive Maximum PUBLISH packets without receiving acknowledgements for them
MUST NOT send more than Receive Maximum QoS 1 and QoS 2 PUBLISH packets for which the Server has not received PUBACK, PUBCOMP, or PUBREC with a Reason Code of 128 or greater from the Client
MUST NOT delay the sending of any packets other than PUBLISH packets due to having sent Receive Maximum PUBLISH packets without receiving acknowledgements for them
is an error: is a Protocol Error for a PUBLISH packet to contain any Subscription Identifier other than those received in SUBSCRIBE packets which caused the PUBLISH packet to flow
is a Protocol Error and the receiver uses DISCONNECT with Reason Code of 0x82 (Protocol Error)
when X, Y shall: When Clients make subscriptions with Topic Filters that include wildcards, it is possible for a Client's subscriptions to overlap so that a published message might match multiple filters. In this case the Server MUST deliver the message to the Client respecting the maximum QoS of all the matching subscriptions

If the Client specified a Subscription Identifier for any of the overlapping subscriptions the Server MUST send those Subscription Identifiers in the message which is published as the result of the subscriptions
If the Server sends a single copy of the message it MUST include in the PUBLISH packet the Subscription Identifiers for all matching subscriptions which have Subscription Identifiers
If the Server sends multiple PUBLISH packets it MUST send, in each of them, the Subscription Identifier of the matching subscription if it has a Subscription Identifier
If the subscription was shared, then only the Subscription Identifiers that were present in the SUBSCRIBE packet from the Client which is receiving the message are returned in the PUBLISH packet

If the receiver has already established a mapping for the Topic Alias, then a) If the packet has a zero length Topic Name, the receiver processes the packet using the Topic Name that corresponds to the Topic Alias b) If the packet contains a non-zero length Topic Name, the receiver processes the packet using that Topic Name and updates its mapping for the Topic Alias to the Topic Name from the incoming packet

If the receiver does not already have a mapping for this Topic Alias a) If the packet has a zero length Topic Name field it is a Protocol Error and the receiver uses DISCONNECT with Reason Code of 0x82 (Protocol Error) b) If the packet contains a Topic Name with a non-zero length, the receiver processes the packet using that Topic Name and sets its mapping for the Topic Alias to the Topic Name from the incoming packet

If the Server receives more than Receive Maximum QoS 1 and QoS 2 PUBLISH packets where the Server has not sent a PUBACK or PUBCOMP in response, the Server uses a DISCONNECT packet with Reason Code 0x93 (Receive Maximum exceeded)

If the Client receives more than Receive Maximum QoS 1 and QoS 2 PUBLISH packets where the Client has not sent a PUBACK or PUBCOMP in response, the Client uses DISCONNECT with Reason Code 0x93 (Receive Maximum exceeded)
failure to do X will result in Y: A Topic Alias value of 0 or greater than the Maximum Topic Alias is a Protocol Error, the receiver uses DISCONNECT with Reason Code of 0x94 (Topic Alias invalid)

mandatory: A Shared Subscription's Topic Filter MUST start with share/ and MUST contain a ShareName that is at least one character long [MQTT-4.8.2-1]
The ShareName MUST NOT contain the characters "/", "+" or "#", but MUST be followed by a "/" character
The "/" character after ShareName MUST be followed by a Topic Filter [MQTT-4.8.2-2]
When sending an Application Message to a Client, the Server MUST respect the granted QoS for the Client's subscription [MQTT-4.8.2-3]

If the Server is in the process of sending a QoS 2 message to its chosen subscribing Client and the connection to the Client breaks before delivery is complete, the Server MUST complete the delivery of the message to that Client when it reconnects [MQTT-4.8.2-4]
If the Client's Session terminates before the Client reconnects, the Server MUST NOT send the Application Message to any other subscribed Client [MQTT-4.8.2-5]

If a Client responds with a PUBACK or PUBREC containing a Reason Code of 0x80 or greater to a PUBLISH packet from the Server, the Server MUST discard the Application Message and not attempt to send it to any other Subscriber [MQTT-4.8.2-6]
prohibited: The ShareName MUST NOT contain the characters "/", "+" or "#"
If the Client's Session terminates before the Client reconnects, the Server MUST NOT send the Application Message to any other subscribed Client [MQTT-4.8.2-5]
recommended: If the Server is in the process of sending a QoS 1 message to its chosen subscribing Client and the connection to that Client breaks before the Server has received an acknowledgement from the Client, the Server MAY wait for the Client to reconnect and retransmit the message to that Client. If the Client's Session terminates before the Client reconnects, the Server SHOULD send the Application Message to another Client that is subscribed to the same Shared Subscription
